enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs about his day in Boulder on January 24, 2017, describing progress on a web project converting numbered UI elements into expanded visual formats, his sleep schedule shifting back to daytime, and starting the 'Revolutions' history podcast after finishing some Roman history podcasts.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: impressed with its speed, feels it handles his editing workload well
thinks he probably should but hasn't committed to it yet

stances.topic: revisiting old work on his website project
stances.position: finds it trippy and disorienting, like time-traveling back to fix old bad norms

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: On January 24, 2017, I studied the Old Testament and New Testament.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I worked on converting Autotest data, where each number box expands to show more details.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: There are over a thousand auditors in the Autotest data.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The computer is slow, so processing the data takes time.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I feel like I can edit the data for selling purposes.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I think the computer should have a second job or maybe I should research better computers.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I made a lot of progress on the Autotest data, currently on book eight of nine.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My sleep schedule switched back to daytime recently.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I worked until 11:45 PM last night.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I woke up around six or seven in the morning.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: After working on auditors, I moved on to another big task.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The data conversion process feels trippy, like traveling back in time to fix old norms.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I listened to Roman history podcasts but couldn't remember their names.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I started listening to a podcast about revolutions, specifically the 1650s, which has 354 episodes.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm really hungry now and plan to have breakfast with protein powder.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm going to eat and then end the video.

To do Herodotus.
